cards.text:
Characters Introduced
- Lambok and his elder sister Phiban are the main characters.
- They live in Shillong with their parents who are at work during the day.

Setting of the Story
- The events unfold when Lambok and Phiban return home from school.
- Their parents are not present as they are working.

Lambok's Condition
- Lambok feels feverish upon returning home.
- Phiban checks him by touching his forehead.

Use of a Thermometer
  • Phiban decides to use a thermometer to confirm Lambok's temperature.
  • The thermometer is stored in their almirah.

Cleaning Process
  • Before measuring, Phiban washes the thermometer tip with soap and water.
  • Cleaning ensures that any prior germs do not affect the reading.

Temperature Check
- Phiban measures Lambok's temperature and finds it normal.

Post-Check Protocol
  • After use, the thermometer tip is washed again, dried, and put back in its place.
  • This is done to maintain hygiene and ensure accurate readings next time.

Phiban's Care for Lambok
- Phiban instructs Lambok to change out of his school uniform.
- She ensures he eats lunch and suggests resting afterwards.

Understanding Temperature Perception
- It highlights that sense of touch is not always reliable for determining temperature.
- Using a thermometer provides more accuracy.

Activity 7.1: Let us Investigate
- The passage emphasizes investigating how certain observations can often lead to misconceptions without proper tools.
A reliable measure of hotness (or coldness) of a body is its temperature.
A device that measures temperature is called a thermometer.

There are two kinds of thermometers that you are likely to come across - clinical thermometers and laboratory thermometers. Clinical thermometers are used to measure human body temperatures whereas laboratory thermometers are used for many other purposes.

Understanding Temperature
Temperature indicates how hot or cold a body is compared to another.

Comparison of Temperature
A hotter body exhibits a higher temperature than a colder one.

Role of Thermometers
Devices that ascertain temperature are known as thermometers.

Types of Thermometers
  • Clinical Thermometers: Used to measure human body temperature.
  • Laboratory Thermometers: Used for various other temperature measurements.

Clinical Thermometer Usage
Specifically designed for accurately measuring human body temperature.

Laboratory Thermometer Functions
Employed in different scientific and industrial contexts for precise temperature assessment.

Purpose of Clinical Thermometers
Crucial in gauging body temperature to monitor health conditions.

Scientific Applications of Laboratory Thermometers
Vital for experiments and procedures that require exact temperature monitoring.

Choosing the Right Thermometer
Select according to requirement - Clinical for medical use and Laboratory for other scientific uses.

Importance of Temperature Measurement
  1. Determines comparative heat levels of different substances or bodies.
  2. Aids in maintaining desired conditions across various applications.
The clinical thermometers generally use a scale called the Celsius scale. On this scale, the unit of temperature is degree Celsius and is denoted by oC.

There is another scale of temperature known as Fahrenheit scale. On this scale, the unit of temperature is degree Fahrenheit and is denoted by oF. A temperature measured as 37.0 oC on Celsius scale is equivalent to 98.6 oF on Fahrenheit scale.
There is another scale of temperature known as Kelvin scale. On this scale, the unit for temperature is kelvin and is denoted by K. The SI unit of temperature is kelvin.

Clinical Thermometers
Clinical thermometers measure body temperature. They are digital, display temperatures easily, and use batteries.

Celsius Scale
The Celsius scale is used in clinical thermometers, denoting temperature in degree Celsius (°C).

Mercury vs. Digital Thermometers
Digital thermometers replace mercury ones due to ease of reading numbers and reduced toxicity risk.

Non-contact Thermometers
Infrared thermometers measure temperature without contact, minimizing disease spread risk.

Normal Human Body Temperature
A healthy human's average body temperature is considered 37.0 °C.

Factors Influencing Body Temperature
  • Age
  • Time of day
  • Activity level

Body Temperature Ranges
Normal body temperature ranges between 35 °C and 42 °C.

Thermometer Placement Variations
Armpit measurements with digital thermometers read 0.5°C to 1°C lower.

Fahrenheit and Kelvin Scales
Fahrenheit (°F) and Kelvin (K) scales exist alongside Celsius for measuring temperature.

Temperature Scale Naming
Temperature scales use capital letters, e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin.

Boiling and Freezing Points
A clinical thermometer cannot measure extremes like boiling water or ice.

Laboratory Thermometers
These have sealed glass tubes with a liquid, usually alcohol or mercury, and are used to measure temperature in labs.

Reading Thermometers
  • Hold vertically
  • Ensure eye level with liquid column
  • Bulb shouldn't touch surfaces

Weather Measurement
Weather stations monitor air temperature to help forecast weather.

Anna Mani's Contributions
Anna Mani developed weather instruments and explored renewable energy in India.
The temperature of a body tells us how hot or cold it is.
A clinical thermometer is used for measuring body temperature.
Normal temperature of a healthy human adult is taken to be 37.0 oC or 98.6 oF.
Laboratory thermometers typically have a temperature range from -10 oC to 110 oC.
